checks.a_analysis: I compared the text in each section of the output against the corresponding pages in the source PDF. The title, headings, and body text provided in the JSON (for pages 1, 2, 3, 4, 20, and 21) accurately reflect the content of the source document. There is no evidence of invented or hallucinated text.

The source document contains one email address (Josh.Meredith@microsoft.com) on page 2. This email is omitted in the output text, but no alternative or incorrect email is provided. No other contact information (phone numbers, URLs, physical addresses) is present in the output that is not in the source. Therefore, no contact information has been fabricated.

The source PDF contains 21 pages of content. The output JSON contains a placeholder stating that '15 sections omitted for token budget'. Upon review, content from pages 5 through 19 of the source document is entirely missing from the output. This includes an example prompt for the 'Art of Prompting' (page 5), several pages of step-by-step practice instructions (pages 6-7, 15-19), and feature overviews/demos (pages 8-14). This constitutes a major loss of the document's information.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source document contains 21 pages. Pages 5 through 19 include specific content like the 'Art of Prompting' example prompt, practice exercises for summarization and drafting, and an overview of Copilot features in Outlook.
checks.b_evidence_location: The omission occurs between section index 2 (page 3) and section index 4 (page 4), although the placement of the omission tag relative to page numbers is inconsistent with the provided reading order indices. Regardless, the content of pages 5-19 is not present.

checks.a_analysis: There are two tables in the output. The first (reading_order 21) maps the four key elements of prompting from page 4 of the source; all four elements are correctly captured. The second (reading_order 143) maps the 12 email prompts shown in a grid on page 20 of the source; all 12 prompts are present across the header and data rows. No rows are missing from either table.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: I evaluated the sections that were included in the output. On page 2, the email address 'Josh.Meredith@microsoft.com' was omitted from the text, which represents approximately 20% of the textual content for that specific bio section. The 'Art of Prompting' (page 4) and 'Copilot Lab' (page 20) sections were captured completely. While page 2 has a minor omission, it does not reach the 25% threshold for a MAJOR defect, and the larger scale omissions of entire pages are already captured under check C3.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text present in the output accurately matches the wording and meaning of the source document. There are no changes to specific terms, numbers, or phrasing that would alter the information provided to a user.

checks.a_analysis: The output was reviewed for any repeated text blocks or sections not present in the source. While slide titles like 'Art of Prompting - Key Elements' appear on consecutive pages (4 and 5) in the source, the JSON snippets correctly reflect the slide-based organization without generating redundant copies of unique content. No unintended duplications were found in the converted output.

The heading hierarchy consistently uses H1 for the main title of each slide (e.g., 'Copilot in Outlook', 'Topics', 'Questions'). This is a standard and acceptable normalization for presentation slides to ensure each slide is uniquely identifiable for screen reader users. No heading levels are skipped (e.g., H1 to H3), and the hierarchy remains logical and stable throughout the provided sections.

The document contains two major grid-like structures (the 'Key Elements' cards on page 4 and the 'Copilot Lab' prompts on page 20) which have been correctly converted into tables. On page 4, the top row of labels ('WHAT do I want?', etc.) is correctly designated as a header row, and the descriptive content is in the data row. On page 20, the 4x3 grid of cards is faithfully represented as a table with 12 cells, preserving the logical grouping of text within each cell. No structural corruption such as merged columns or misplaced headers was found.

The reading order follows the logical flow of the PowerPoint presentation, progressing slide by slide. Within individual slides, titles are read first, followed by main content (lists or tables), and ending with branding or background images. This order is consistent with the source's intended information flow and is optimized for accessibility.

The list on page 3 ('Topics') has been correctly converted into an unordered list structure in the JSON. The number of items (5) and their textual content match the source perfectly. No nesting errors or merging of items were detected.

The email address provided as a contact link on page 2 ('Josh.Meredith@microsoft.com') is entirely missing from the output. The paragraph sequence in the output for that slide ends with the 'State and Local Government division' text, omitting the final line of contact information present in the source PDF. According to the audit criteria, missing contact links (email, phone) are considered a defect.
checks.b_evidence_output: The text and associated link are missing from the paragraph elements in Section 2.
checks.b_evidence_location: Slide 2, Section 2 (index 1), following 'State and Local Government division' (reading_order 8)

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output correctly translates the PowerPoint slides into a linear web document. The visual organization of information matches the source PDF exactly in terms of content flow and sequence. A user can find all information in the same relative order as the original document. Responsive adjustments (collapsing side-by-side elements into a vertical stack) are within the acceptable scope of HTML conversion.

The source document does not contain traditional complex data tables. Grid-based layouts used for presentation (such as the 'Art of Prompting' elements on Page 4) are rendered clearly in the HTML, maintaining readability and structural integrity.

All meaningful images from the source PDF have been successfully migrated to the HTML. This includes the profile photos and lifestyle images on Page 2, the Copilot logo on Page 8, the various software screenshots on Pages 9, 11, 12, 13, and 14, and the grid of prompt examples on Page 20. No meaningful visual content is missing.

A thorough visual inspection of the rendered HTML confirms there is no duplication of body content. Each slide's content appears exactly once in the main body. The presence of headings in the navigation menu/TOC is expected accessible design and does not constitute duplication.
